mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity risk-off flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains below -0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and investment demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint decline in HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a classification accuracy above 52%, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects restored manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to generate positive alpha during recovery regimes, with mean forward returns statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-stat < 1.5) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation measured by CPIAUCSL falls below 2.0 percent year-over-year, markets price in imminent monetary accommodation, triggering positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain negative or flat when CPIAUCSL growth drops below 2.0%, or the signal hit rate falls below 45% across multiple disinflationary episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 10-year out-of-sample period, or Rank IC consistently remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP coinciding with falling housing starts signals a broad-based demand shock that forces equity repricing, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The interaction between GDPC1 and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance, or the model out-of-sample R-squared drops below 0.02 during credit stress periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent indicates immediate supply-side weakness that triggers short-term equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where INDPRO falls by over 0.5%, or the threshold signal loses predictive power (p > 0.05) in walk-forward testing.
